summary: Students can start quiz attempts before the configured open_at time and continue answering or submit after the configured due_at time because the attempt workflow never enforces quiz availability windows. This allows bypass of intended release and deadline controls for assessments.

evidence_snippets.reason: The application stores explicit quiz opening and due times, indicating that quiz availability windows are part of the intended workflow.
The UI presents open_at and due_at to users as meaningful quiz state constraints.
Attempt creation checks only that the quiz exists and does not enforce open_at or due_at before allowing a student to start.
Answer submission enforces only ownership and submitted_at status; it never checks whether the quiz is not yet open or already past due.
Final submission likewise omits any check against the quiz's configured due_at deadline.

root_cause: The quiz attempt lifecycle stores schedule metadata on quizzes but does not enforce those state-transition rules when students start, answer, or submit attempts.
repair_plan: Before allowing attempt creation, answer saving, or final submission, load the quiz and enforce its availability rules server-side: reject starts before open_at, reject answering or submitting after due_at, and define whether in-progress attempts may continue past due_at. Apply the same checks consistently to both HTML and API attempt endpoints.
